chapter_title.en: Chapter 16 – The Laudation before the supplication

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: موثق كالصحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (12/59)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: موثق كالصحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (12/59)
text: قَالَ أَبُو عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) إِنَّ فِي كِتَابِ أَمِيرِ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ ( صلوات الله عليه )  إِنَّ الْمِدْحَةَ قَبْلَ الْمَسْأَلَةِ فَإِذَا دَعَوْتَ اللَّهَ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ فَمَجِّدْهُ قُلْتُ كَيْفَ أُمَجِّدُهُ قَالَ تَقُولُ.

يَا مَنْ هُوَ أَقْرَبُ إِلَيَّ مِنْ حَبْلِ الْوَرِيدِ يَا فَعَّالًا لِمَا يُرِيدُ يَا مَنْ يَحُولُ بَيْنَ الْمَرْءِ وَ قَلْبِهِ يَا مَنْ هُوَ بِالْمَنْظَرِ الْأَعْلَى يَا مَنْ هُوَ لَيْسَ كَمِثْلِهِ شَيْ‏ءٌ

en.hubeali: Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad Bin Isa, from Ibn Fazzal, from Ibn Bukeyr, from Muhammad Bin Muslim who said,

‘Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘In the Book of Amir Al-Momineen<sup>asws</sup>, the eulogising is before the asking. Therefore, whenever you supplicate to Allah<sup>azwj</sup> Mighty and Majestic, so Glorify Him<sup>azwj</sup>’. I said, ‘How shall I Glorfiy Him<sup>azwj</sup>?’ He<sup>asws</sup> said:

‘O the One Who is closer to me than the jugular vein! O the One Who Does whatever He<sup>azwj</sup> Wants to! O the One Who Turns around between the man and his heart! O the One Who is in the Exalted viewpoint! O the One Who, there is nothing like Him<sup>azwj</sup>!’’.
en.sarwar: 2. Muhammad ibn Yahya has narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad ibn ‘Isa from ibn Faddal from ibn Bukayr from Muhammad ibn Muslim who has said the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah (a.s.) has said, ‘It is in the book of Amir al-Mu’minin, Ali ibn abu Talib (a.s.) “praise comes before asking for favors. Whenever you like to pray and plead before Allah, the Most Majestic, the Most Holy, for help, speak of His glory.” The narrator has said that I asked the Imam, ‘How should we speak of His glory?’ The Imam said, ‘Say, O You Who are closer to me than my jugular veins, O the One Who does without fail as He wishes, O the One Who stands between a man and his heart, O the One Who is on a high position and the One like Whom is no creature.”’
